description: Auditor binding spec for the case-law citation credibility gate over a VERBATIM-rooted court-record corpus (Axis-2 spec-pinned dispatch). The representative re-derived output is the gate verdict object {decision, citations:[{id, reporter_cite, status}]} from outputs/gate_verdict.json. It is recomputed by resolving each asserted reporter cite against the rooted corpus (corpus/rooted_records.json) and testing the producer's quoted holding for verbatim membership in the resolved record's rooted_text -- the court's actual opinion text captured from CourtListener with provenance (assertions/citation_assertions.json): absent cite -> UNRESOLVED, resolved-but-quote-not-found-verbatim -> MISQUOTE, else ROOTED; decision = AUTO_APPROVE iff every citation is ROOTED else ROUTE_TO_HUMAN. The resolve + verbatim-misquote rule lives in the verifier primitive (caselaw_gate_kb_recompute); the comparator is pinned HERE by the auditor (exact, no params -- the whole verdict object compared element-wise), not producer-asserted. A producer who ships a spec the auditor did not anchor (different SHA / different primitive_id / weaker comparator) gets a SHA the anchor does not list, so dispatch fails closed. The auditor anchors the METHOD; that each rooted_text is the genuine, complete opinion for its cite is a trust-root (L-pillar) concern, mitigated by per-record CourtListener provenance and documented in the pilot README.
